response.justification: Exceptional alignment on AI-native environment and distributed systems expertise; currently working as Staff Engineer on agentic tooling at re:cinq, which is nearly perfect role context. However, Python is explicitly listed as a must-have 'strong' skill and is notably absent from the resume, despite demonstrated ability to acquire languages. Operational ownership experience is not articulated clearly. Would benefit from clarification on Python capabilities during screening.
strengths: Currently Staff Engineer at AI infrastructure company building agent tooling and MCP frameworks—direct experience with agentic systems and the exact technical environment of the role
Proven high-throughput distributed systems expertise: led low-latency, high-concurrency systems handling millions of requests per second with async I/O, caching, and performance databases

Strong ownership mindset across multiple dimensions: founded and maintains open-source platform (GISCollective), transitioned into backend when iOS dev departed (WatchUp), built CI/CD and infrastructure (Altom, National Instruments), mentored through workshops and code katas
concerns: Python not listed in skills despite being explicitly required as a 'must-have' with strong proficiency; while resume shows ability to acquire languages, this gap should be verified directly
Operational readiness and incident-response capability not clearly demonstrated; no on-call rotation, incident command, or SRE-adjacent experience articulated in role descriptions
Stakeholder management and cross-functional coordination not explicitly evidenced; roles focus on technical execution without clear examples of bridging engineering and product perspectives

key_factors.factor: Current AI infrastructure role at Staff level
Distributed systems and high-throughput architecture depth
